creator: diizzy@FreeBSD.org
text:
Less (manual) hacking/patching overall

Makefile overrides framework flags in general (WITH_DEBUG is also broken), install path of .pc file needs patching), stripping needs to be done in port Makefile, verbose build needs manual defintion because it's overriden by default to name a few issues

Building is slower especially when running "make test", possibly flags are being lost but again... more hacking/patching involved

CMake vs gmake (make)
        3.16s real              7.16s user              0.67s sys
        4.87s real              17.16s user             0.76s sys

CMake vs gmake (make test)
        21.11s real             1m11.93s user           14.96s sys
        1m3.93s real            1m4.05s user            13.98s sys

fwiw, both consumers are also pulling in CMake so I don't see this as an issue not to mention it integrates more "seamlessly"/better with the ports framework overall

Best regards,
Daniel
